function_codes.display.nphDesign:
display.nphDesign = function(nphDesign=D0.A18X1.W1, maxT=50){

  ##############
  #Extract the setting parameters from nphDesign object if provided
  ##############
  nph = f.extract(nphDesign)
  n = nph$N; A=nph$A;  w=nph$w; r=nph$r; 
  lambda0=nph$lam0; lambda1 = nph$lam1;
  cuts = nph$cuts; targetEvents = nph$targetEvents; 
  
  overall.alpha = nph$overall.alpha;
  side = nph$side; alpha = nph$alpha; f.ws = nph$fws

  h0 = nphDesign$design$setting$h0
  S0 = nphDesign$design$setting$S0  
  h1 = nphDesign$design$setting$h1
  S1 = nphDesign$design$setting$S1
  Fentry = nphDesign$design$setting$F.entry
  G.ltfu = nphDesign$design$setting$G.ltfu
  f.logHR = nphDesign$design$setting$log.HR
  
  #Number of analyses
  K = length(f.ws)
  J = lengths(f.ws) 
  timing = targetEvents/targetEvents[K]
  
  #(1) Accrual curve
  t = seq(0, A, by = 1)
  cum.enroll = Fentry(t)
  plot(t, cum.enroll, type="l", lwd=3, xlab = "Calendar Time", ylab="Cumulative Accrual")
  abline(v=seq(0, A, 1), col="gray80", lty=3)
  abline(h=seq(0, 1, 0.1), col="gray80", lty=3)
  
  #(2) Display distributions
  plot_S(S=list(S0, S1), Tmax = maxT)
  
  #(3) Display cumulative events over time
  plot_events(Tmax = maxT, r=r, h0=h0, S0=S0, h1=h1, S1=S1, 
              F.entry = Fentry, G.ltfu = G.ltfu, n = n)
  
  #(4) Display Expected Average hazard ratio over calendar time
  plot_AHR(r = r, n =n, h0 = h0, S0=S0, h1 = h1, S1=S1, f.logHR = f.logHR,
           rho = 0, gamma = 0, tau = NULL, s.tau = 0, f.ws = NULL,
           F.entry = Fentry, G.ltfu = G.ltfu, Tmax=maxT)
  
  #(5) Non-centrality parameter curves
  plot_wlr.mu (Tmax = maxT, r = r, n = n,h0 = h0, S0= S0, h1 = h1, S1=S1, 
               f.logHR = f.logHR, fws = f.ws[[K]], F.entry = Fentry, G.ltfu = G.ltfu)
  
  #(6) Projected z boundary curves
  plot(nphDesign$design$events$events, nphDesign$design$bounds[,1], type="b", lwd=2, 
       xlab = "Analysis Timing Based on Events", ylab = "z Rejection boundary", 
       main = "Projected Rejection Boundary (Z)")
  abline(v = seq(0, nphDesign$design$events$events[K]*2, 10), col="gray80", lty=3)
  abline(h = seq(0, 10, 0.05), col="gray80", lty=3)

  e0 =   nphDesign$design$events$events[K]
  #(7) Power curve vs events 
  e.seq = seq(max(10, e0/50), min(e0*1.2, n*0.85), length.out = 30)
  overall.pow.seq = rep(NA, length(e.seq))
  pow.seq = matrix(NA, nrow=length(e.seq), ncol=K)
  
  for (i in 1:length(e.seq)){
    wlr.seq = wlr.power.maxcombo(T = NULL, events = e.seq[i] * timing, 
                          alpha = alpha, power = NULL, side = side, r = r, n = n, 
                          h0 = h0, S0=S0, h1 = h1, S1= S1, f.logHR = f.logHR, 
                          f.ws = f.ws, F.entry=Fentry, G.ltfu=G.ltfu)
    overall.pow.seq[i] = wlr.seq$overall.power
    pow.seq[i,] = wlr.seq$power
  }
  #Overall power
  plot(e.seq, overall.pow.seq, type="l", lwd=3, xlab="Number of Events at Final Analysis", 
       ylab="Power", main="Overall Power")
  
  abline(h = seq(0, 1, 0.05), col="gray80", lty=3)
  abline(v = seq(0, max(e.seq), by=10), col="gray80", lty=3)
  
  abline(v=e0*timing, col="green", lwd=3)
  
  #Power of each analysis
  for(j in 1:K){
    plot(e.seq*timing[j], pow.seq[,j], type="l", lwd=3, xlab = paste("Number of Events at Analysis", j), 
         ylab="Power", main=paste("Power of analysis", j))
    
    abline(h = seq(0, 1, 0.05), col="gray80", lty=3)
    abline(v = seq(0, max(e.seq*timing[j]), by=10), col="gray80", lty=3)
    
    abline(v=e0*timing[j], col="green", lwd=3)
  }
  
  #(8) P-value boundary for non-combination test at each analysis
  if (sum(J) == K){
    if(side == 1){
      p.bd = 1 - pnorm(nphDesign$design$bounds[,1])
    }
    if (side == 2){
      p.bd = 2*(1 - pnorm(nphDesign$design$bounds[,1]))
    }
    plot(nphDesign$design$events$events, p.bd, type="b", lwd=2, 
         xlab = "Analysis Timing Based on Events", ylab="P value", 
         main = "Projected Rejection Boundary (p-value)")
    abline(v = seq(0, nphDesign$design$events$events[K]*2, 10), col="gray80", lty=3)
    abline(h = seq(0, 1, 0.001), col="gray80", lty=3)
  }
  
}
function_codes.display.simulation:
display.simulation = function(s.nphDesign){

  K = length(s.nphDesign$power)
  nSim = dim(s.nphDesign$wlr.simulations)[1]
  
  #Tabulate power
  colnm = NULL
  for (i in 1:K){
    ai = paste("Analysis", i)
    colnm = c(colnm, ai)
  }
    
  knitr::kable(round(s.nphDesign$power*100,2), col.names = colnm, 
               caption="Power for Each Analysis (%)", 
               format = "markdown")
  knitr::kable(round(s.nphDesign$overall.power*100,2), col.names ="Overall Power", 
               caption="Overall Study Power", format = "markdown")
  
  #Power curve over analysis
  pow = s.nphDesign$power; 
  overall.pow = s.nphDesign$overall.power
  plot(1:K, pow, ylim=c(0, 1), 
       type="n", xlim=c(0, K+1), xlab="Analysis", ylab="Power",
       main=paste("Power by simulations: ", nSim, "trials"))
  for  (i in 1:K){
    points(i, pow[i], pch=i, col=i, lwd=3, cex=2)
  }  
  points(K+1, overall.pow, pch=3, col="maroon", lwd=4, cex=2)
  
  legend(0, 0.3, c("Overall Power"), pch=3, col="maroon", bty="n")
  
  #Density curve of z values
  for (i in 1:K){
    fit = density(s.nphDesign$wlr.simulations[,1,i,1])
    plot(fit, ylab="z Values", 
         main = paste("z based on", nSim, "simulations for Analysis", i))
    abline(v=s.nphDesign$wlr.simulations[1,1,i,4], col="green", lwd=3)
    legend(min(fit$x), max(fit$y), c("Bound"), bty="n", col="green", lwd=3, lty=1, cex=0.8)
  }
  
  #If logrank test is requested, then additionally display
  if(!is.null(s.nphDesign$lr.power)){
    lr.pow = s.nphDesign$lr.power; 
    lr.overall.pow = s.nphDesign$lr.overall.power
    plot(1:K, lr.pow, ylim=c(0, 1), 
         type="n", xlim=c(0, K+1), xlab="Analysis", ylab="Power",
         main=paste("Logrank Power by simulations: ", nSim, "trials"))
    for  (i in 1:K){
      points(i, lr.pow[i], pch=i, col=i, lwd=3, cex=2)
    }  
    points(K+1, lr.overall.pow, pch=3, col="maroon", lwd=4, cex=2)
    
    legend(0, 0.3, c("Overall Logrank Power"), pch=3, col="maroon", bty="n")
    
    #Density curve of z values
    for (i in 1:K){
      fit = density(s.nphDesign$lr.simulations[,i,1])
      plot(fit, ylab="z Values", 
           main = paste("Logrank z based on", nSim, "simulations for Analysis", i))
      abline(v=s.nphDesign$lr.simulations[1,i,4], col="green", lwd=3)
      legend(min(fit$x), max(fit$y), c("Bound"), bty="n", col="green", lwd=3, lty=1, cex=0.8)
    }
    
  }
}
function_codes.display.stat.setting:
display.stat.setting = function(dist = PFS.dist, accr = study.accr, 
                                alphabeta = PFS.alphabeta, test = PFS.test,
                                caption="Summary of Accrual and Statistical Setting"){
  d = data.frame(cbind(dist))
  d[row.names(d)=="control", 1] = paste(dist$control$dist, "distribution with median", dist$control$median)
  if (dist$exp$delay==0){exp.d = paste("Exponential distribution with HR", dist$exp$HR)} else{exp.d = paste("Delayed effect: ", dist$exp$delay, "and the HR after delay:", dist$exp$HR)}
  d[row.names(d)=="exp", 1] = exp.d
  
  a = data.frame(cbind(accr))
  ab = data.frame(cbind(alphabeta))
  t = data.frame(cbind(test))
  
  d2 = rename(d, Description = dist)
  a2 = rename(a, Description = accr)
  ab2 = rename(ab, Description = alphabeta)
  t2 = rename(t, Description = test)
  
  d2$stat = "Statistical Distribution"
  a2$stat = "Assumed Study Accrual"
  ab2$stat = "Type I (Alpha) and Type II Error (Beta)"
  t2$stat = "Statistical Test"
  
  out = data.frame(rbind(a2, d2, ab2, t2))
  out$label = row.names(out)
  
  tab = out  %>% group_by(stat)  %>% gt()
  tab = tab  %>% cols_move(columns = c(Description), after = label)
  tab = tab %>% tab_header(
    title = md(caption), 
  ) %>% tab_footnote(
    footnote = "The unit for median and accrual is month.",
    locations = cells_column_labels(columns = Description)
  )
  return(tab)
}
function_codes.f.nEachMonth:
f.nEachMonth = function (N=600, A=24, w=2, r=2) {
  
  N1 = N * (r/(r+1))
  N0 = N - N1
  
  #When r > 1, the control arm has smaller number of pts. 
  #Just need to determine enrollment for control arm per month, 
  #then to obtain enrollment for experimental arm by n1i = n0i * r.
  
  n1 = n0 = rep(NA, A) #enrollment by month
  randdt1 = rep(NA, N1) #randomization date
  randdt0 = rep(NA, N0)
  
  #Determine number of pts per month for control arm
  #(i-1)th month cumulative enrolled pts
  cLastN0 = 0
  for (i in 1:A) {
    #ith month: cumulative #pts
    cN0i = max(round((i/A)^w * N0), 1)
    
    n0[i] = max(cN0i - cLastN0, 1)
    if (i == A) {n0[i] = N0 - sum(n0[1:(A-1)]) }
    cLastN0 = cN0i  
  }
  n1 = n0 * r
  
  o = list()
  o$n0 = n0
  o$n1 = n1
  return(o)
}

function_codes.wlr.cov2t:
wlr.cov2t = function(time1=c(5,7,10,12,12,15,20,20), event1=c(1,0,0,1,1,0,1,1),
                     time2=c(5,10,13,12,14,15,20,20), event2=c(1,0,1,1,1,1,1,1),
                     group=c(0,1,0,1,0,1,0,1), strata1=NULL, strata2=NULL, strata3=NULL, 
                     rho1=0, gamma1=0, tau1 = NULL, s.tau1=0.5,
                     rho2=0, gamma2=1, tau2 = NULL, s.tau2=0.5,
                     f.ws1=NULL, f.ws2=NULL) {
  
  #Initialize strata1-3
  n = length(time1)
  if(is.null(strata1)){strata1 = rep(1,n)}
  if(is.null(strata2)){strata2 = rep(1,n)}
  if(is.null(strata3)){strata3 = rep(1,n)}
  u.strata1 = unique(strata1)
  u.strata2 = unique(strata2)
  u.strata3 = unique(strata3)
  
  n.strata1 = length(u.strata1)
  n.strata2 = length(u.strata2)
  n.strata3 = length(u.strata3)
  n.strata = n.strata1*n.strata2*n.strata3
  
  #unstratified analysis
  if (n.strata == 1) {
    o = wlr.cov2t0(time1=time1, event1=event1, time2=time2, event2=event2, group=group, 
                   rho1=rho1, gamma1=gamma1, tau1 = tau1, 
                   s.tau1=s.tau1, rho2=rho2, gamma2=gamma2, tau2 = tau2, 
                   s.tau2=s.tau2,f.ws1=f.ws1,f.ws2=f.ws2)
  }else{
    uni.event.time1=uni.event.time2=data=NULL; cov=0
    for (i in 1:n.strata1) {
      for (j in 1:n.strata2){
        for (k in 1:n.strata3){
          #Identifier of the unique stratum in combination of strata1, strata2, and strata3
          ix = (strata1==u.strata1[i] & strata2 == u.strata2[j] & strata3 == u.strata3[k])
            #Calculation for non-empty strata
            if(sum(ix) != 0){
            #survival data at 1st analysis within each stratum
            time1.s = time1[ix]
            event1.s = event1[ix]
            #survival data at 2nd analysis within each stratum
            time2.s = time2[ix]
            event2.s = event2[ix]
            group.s = group[ix]
            #Find the covariance within each stratum
            wlr.s = wlr.cov2t0(time1=time1.s, event1=event1.s, time2=time2.s, event2=event2.s, group=group.s, 
                             rho1=rho1, gamma1=gamma1, tau1 = tau1, 
                             s.tau1=s.tau1, rho2=rho2, gamma2=gamma2, tau2 = tau2, 
                             s.tau2=s.tau2,f.ws1=f.ws1,f.ws2=f.ws2)
            #Record the strata variables
            wlr.s$uni.event.time1$strata1 = u.strata1[i]
            wlr.s$uni.event.time1$strata2 = u.strata2[j]
            wlr.s$uni.event.time1$strata3 = u.strata3[k]
          
            wlr.s$uni.event.time2$strata1 = u.strata1[i]
            wlr.s$uni.event.time2$strata2 = u.strata2[j]
            wlr.s$uni.event.time2$strata3 = u.strata3[k]
          
            uni.event.time1 = rbind(uni.event.time1, wlr.s$uni.event.time1)
            uni.event.time2 = rbind(uni.event.time2, wlr.s$uni.event.time2)
          
            wlr.s$data$strata1 = u.strata1[i];
            wlr.s$data$strata2 = u.strata2[j]
            wlr.s$data$strata3 = u.strata3[k]
            data = rbind(data, wlr.s$data);
          
            cov = cov + wlr.s$cov
          }
        }  
      }       
    }  
    
    corr = cov[1,2]/(sqrt(cov[1,1]*cov[2,2]))    
    #create a dataframe to output the other statistics
    total.events1 = sum(event1==1)
    total.events2 = sum(event2==1)
    event.ratio = total.events1/total.events2
    sqrt.event.ratio = sqrt(event.ratio)
    
    summary.events = data.frame(cbind(total.events1, total.events2, event.ratio, sqrt.event.ratio))
    
    o=list()
    o$uni.event.time1 = uni.event.time1
    o$uni.event.time2 = uni.event.time2
    #o$data = data
    o$cov = cov
    o$corr = corr
    o$summary.events = summary.events
  }
  return(o)
}

library(shiny)
library(bslib)
library(nphDesign) # Custom package
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2) # Although display.nphDesign uses base R plots, ggplot2 is often useful.

# Package Name: nphDesign
# Description: The nphDesign package provides functions for designing and analyzing clinical trials
# with non-proportional hazards (NPH), including weighted log-rank tests and average hazard ratio (AHR) methods.

# Helper functions for f.ws parameter (from nphDesign examples)
# CRITICAL RULE: VECTORIZATION (KB 14.4)
# Functions passed to numerical routines (like those implicitly used by nphDesign)
# MUST return a vector of the same length as their input.
lr <- function(s) { rep(1, length(s)) } # Constant, so needs rep()
fh01 <- function(s) { 1 - s } # Already vectorized
fh11 <- function(s) { s * (1 - s) } # Already vectorized

ui <- page_sidebar(
  title = "NPH Design Analysis",
  theme = bs_theme(version = 5, bootswatch = "flatly"),
  sidebar = sidebar(
    title = "Study Design Parameters",
    width = 400,
    accordion(
      accordion_panel(
        "General Settings",
        numericInput("n_total", "Total Sample Size (n)", value = 300, min = 1, step = 1),
        numericInput("r_ratio", "Randomization Ratio (Exp:Control)", value = 1, min = 0.1, step = 0.1),
        radioButtons("side_type", "Side (1=Superiority, 2=Two-sided)", choices = c("1", "2"), selected = "1", inline = TRUE),
        numericInput("maxT_display", "Max Calendar Time for Display (months)", value = 50, min = 1, step = 1)
      ),
      accordion_panel(
        "Distribution Settings",
        selectInput("control_dist_type", "Control Distribution", choices = c("exponential")),
        numericInput("control_median", "Control Median (months)", value = 6.7, min = 0.1, step = 0.1),
        numericInput("exp_HR", "Experimental HR (after delay)", value = 6.7/12.5, min = 0.01, max = 10, step = 0.01),
        numericInput("exp_delay", "Experimental Delay (months)", value = 6, min = 0, step = 0.1),
        radioButtons("crossover_status", "Crossover Status", choices = c("No" = "N", "Yes" = "Y"), selected = "N", inline = TRUE),
        conditionalPanel(
          condition = "input.crossover_status == 'Y'",
          numericInput("crossover_HRx", "Crossover HRx", value = 1, min = 0.01, max = 10, step = 0.01),
          numericInput("crossover_Tx", "Crossover Time Tx (months)", value = 0, min = 0, step = 0.1)
        )
      ),
      accordion_panel(
        "Accrual Settings",
        numericInput("accr_A", "Accrual Duration A (months)", value = 23, min = 0.1, step = 0.1),
        numericInput("accr_xi", "Accrual Power xi", value = 2, min = 0.1, step = 0.1),
        numericInput("accr_LTFU", "Lost-to-Followup Rate", value = 0, min = 0, max = 1, step = 0.01)
      ),
      accordion_panel(
        "Alpha/Beta Settings",
        selectInput("sf_type", "Spending Function Type", choices = c("LDOF", "LDPK", "GAMMA")),
        conditionalPanel(
          condition = "input.sf_type == 'GAMMA'",
          numericInput("sf_param", "SF Gamma Parameter", value = -2, step = 0.1)
        ),
        textInput("alpha_values", "Alpha Values (comma-separated, e.g., 0.01, 0.015)", value = "0.01, 0.015"),
        numericInput("overall_alpha", "Overall Alpha", value = 0.025, min = 0.001, max = 0.5, step = 0.001),
        numericInput("beta_value", "Beta", value = 0.1, min = 0.01, max = 0.5, step = 0.01),
        textInput("timing_values", "Analysis Timing (comma-separated, e.g., 0.75, 1.0)", value = "0.75, 1.0")
      ),
      accordion_panel(
        "Analysis Timing & Weights",
        textInput("T_times", "Analysis Times (calendar months, comma-separated)", value = "29, 45.5"),
        uiOutput("fws_inputs") # Dynamic UI for weight functions
      ),
      accordion_panel(
        "Advanced Settings",
        selectInput("non_centrality_method", "Non-centrality Method", choices = c("Heetal2021"))
      )
    ),
    actionButton("run_analysis", "Run Analysis", class = "btn-primary w-100 mt-3")
  ),
  card(
    card_header("Study Design Graphics"),
    # CRITICAL RULE: BLACK-BOX OUTPUT (KB 14.8)
    # display.nphDesign draws multiple base-R graphics as a side effect.
    # It must be called ONCE inside ONE renderPlot.
    # par(mfrow) will be used to arrange the plots.
    plotOutput("design_plots", height = "1200px") # Increased height for multiple plots
  )
)

server <- function(input, output, session) {

  # Reactive parsing and validation for vector inputs (KB 14.9.B)
  T_parsed <- reactive({
    req(input$T_times)
    vals <- as.numeric(strsplit(input$T_times, ",")[[1]])
    validate(
      need(!any(is.na(vals)), "Analysis times (T) must be comma-separated numbers.")
    )
    vals
  })

  alpha_parsed <- reactive({
    req(input$alpha_values)
    vals <- as.numeric(strsplit(input$alpha_values, ",")[[1]])
    validate(
      need(!any(is.na(vals)), "Alpha values must be comma-separated numbers.")
    )
    vals
  })

  timing_parsed <- reactive({
    req(input$timing_values)
    vals <- as.numeric(strsplit(input$timing_values, ",")[[1]])
    validate(
      need(!any(is.na(vals)), "Analysis timing must be comma-separated numbers.")
    )
    vals
  })

  # Dynamic UI for f.ws (weight functions)
  output$fws_inputs <- renderUI({
    K <- length(timing_parsed())
    req(K > 0)

    weight_choices <- c("Log-Rank (lr)" = "lr", "Fleming-Harrington (0,1) (fh01)" = "fh01", "Fleming-Harrington (1,1) (fh11)" = "fh11")
    
    fws_ui_elements <- lapply(seq_len(K), function(k) {
      analysis_name <- if (k == 1) "IA1" else if (k == K) "FA" else paste0("IA", k)
      default_selected <- if (k == 1) "lr" else c("lr", "fh01") # Example defaults
      
      selectizeInput(
        inputId = paste0("fws_analysis_", k),
        label = paste("Weight Functions for", analysis_name),
        choices = weight_choices,
        selected = default_selected,
        multiple = TRUE,
        options = list(placeholder = "Select weight functions")
      )
    })
    tagList(fws_ui_elements)
  })

  # Reactive to construct the f.ws list (KB 14.7)
  fws_list_reactive <- reactive({
    K <- length(timing_parsed())
    req(K > 0)

    fws_func_map <- list(
      "lr" = lr,
      "fh01" = fh01,
      "fh11" = fh11
    )

    f.ws_list <- list()
    for (k in seq_len(K)) {
      input_id <- paste0("fws_analysis_", k)
      selected_weights_names <- input[[input_id]]
      
      # Ensure input is available and not NULL before proceeding
      if (is.null(selected_weights_names) || length(selected_weights_names) == 0) {
        # Provide a default or error if no weights are selected for an analysis
        # For now, let's make it a validation error if nothing is selected
        validate(
          need(FALSE, paste("Please select at least one weight function for Analysis", k))
        )
      }
      
      analysis_weights <- lapply(selected_weights_names, function(name) {
        fws_func_map[[name]]
      })

      analysis_name <- if (k == 1) "IA1" else if (k == K) "FA" else paste0("IA", k)
      f.ws_list[[analysis_name]] <- analysis_weights
    }
    f.ws_list
  })

  # Reactive to construct the 'dist' list for finalize.nphDesign
  dist_list <- reactive({
    req(input$control_dist_type, input$control_median, input$exp_HR, input$exp_delay, input$crossover_status)
    
    dist_obj <- list(
      control = list(dist = input$control_dist_type, median = input$control_median),
      exp = list(HR = input$exp_HR, delay = input$exp_delay),
      crossover = list(status = input$crossover_status, HRx = NULL, Tx = NULL)
    )
    
    if (input$crossover_status == "Y") {
      req(input$crossover_HRx, input$crossover_Tx)
      dist_obj$crossover$HRx <- input$crossover_HRx
      dist_obj$crossover$Tx <- input$crossover_Tx
    }
    dist_obj
  })

  # Reactive to construct the 'accr' list for finalize.nphDesign
  accr_list <- reactive({
    req(input$accr_A, input$accr_xi, input$accr_LTFU)
    list(
      A = input$accr_A,
      xi = input$accr_xi,
      LTFU = input$accr_LTFU # Note: G.ltfu is derived from this in finalize.nphDesign
    )
  })

  # Reactive to construct the 'alphabeta' list for finalize.nphDesign
  alphabeta_list <- reactive({
    req(input$sf_type, input$overall_alpha, input$beta_value, input$side_type)
    
    sf_list <- list(type = input$sf_type)
    if (input$sf_type == "GAMMA") {
      req(input$sf_param)
      sf_list$param <- input$sf_param
    }

    list(
      sf = sf_list,
      alpha = alpha_parsed(), # Use parsed alpha values
      overall.alpha = input$overall_alpha,
      beta = input$beta_value,
      side = as.numeric(input$side_type),
      timing = timing_parsed() # Use parsed timing values
    )
  })

  # Reactive for the nphDesign object (KB 14.7)
  nphDesign_object <- eventReactive(input$run_analysis, {
    # CRITICAL RULE: VALIDATE LENGTHS (KB 14.9.B)
    # Ensure T, alpha, and timing have consistent lengths before calling.
    validate(
      need(length(T_parsed()) == length(alpha_parsed()),
           "Number of analysis times (T) must match number of alpha values."),
      need(length(T_parsed()) == length(timing_parsed()),
           "Number of analysis times (T) must match number of timing values.")
    )
    
    # CRITICAL RULE: CUSTOM PACKAGE APIS (KB 14.6) & DEPENDENCY CHAINS (KB 14.7)
    # Call finalize.nphDesign with exact argument names and types as per specification.
    # show.setting = "Y" is required for display.nphDesign.
    nphDesign::finalize.nphDesign(
      T = T_parsed(),
      n = input$n_total,
      r = input$r_ratio,
      dist = dist_list(),
      accr = accr_list(),
      alphabeta = alphabeta_list(),
      f.ws = fws_list_reactive(), # Use the dynamically constructed list of functions
      show.setting = "Y", # Required for display.nphDesign
      non.centrality = input$non_centrality_method
    )
  })

  # Render the plots from display.nphDesign
  output$design_plots <- renderPlot({
    req(nphDesign_object())
    
    # CRITICAL RULE: BLACK-BOX OUTPUT (KB 14.8)
    # display.nphDesign generates multiple base R plots.
    # We set par(mfrow) to arrange them in a grid.
    # The number of plots is 6 fixed plots + K plots for "Power of each analysis"
    # + 1 conditional plot for "P-value boundary" (if sum(J)==K).
    # K is length(f.ws).
    K_analyses <- length(nphDesign_object()$design$f.ws)
    
    # Check if p-value boundary plot will be shown (sum(J) == K)
    # J is lengths(f.ws) from source, so sum(J) == K implies each analysis has exactly one weight function.
    # This is not directly exposed in the nphDesign_object, but we can infer it
    # from the fws_list_reactive structure.
    # For simplicity, let's assume the p-value boundary plot is shown if K_analyses > 0 and all analyses have 1 weight.
    # Or, more robustly, check the internal structure if possible.
    # For now, let's assume it's often shown and allocate space.
    
    # Total plots: 6 (fixed) + K_analyses (power per analysis) + 1 (overall power) + 1 (p-value boundary if applicable)
    # The source code for display.nphDesign has 8 explicit plot calls, plus K plots in a loop.
    # 1. Accrual curve
    # 2. plot_S
    # 3. plot_events
    # 4. plot_AHR
    # 5. plot_wlr.mu
    # 6. Z boundary
    # 7. Overall power
    # 8. K plots for power of each analysis
    # 9. P-value boundary (conditional)
    
    # So, 7 + K_analyses + (1 if p-value boundary is shown).
    # Let's assume K_analyses is typically 2 or 3.
    # If K_analyses = 2, total plots = 7 + 2 + (0 or 1) = 9 or 10.
    # A 3x3 grid (9 plots) or 4x3 grid (12 plots) would be suitable.
    # Let's use 3x3 for up to 9 plots, and warn if more.
    
    num_plots_expected <- 7 + K_analyses # Minimum 7 + K_analyses
    
    # The condition `sum(J) == K` for the p-value boundary plot means each analysis
    # has exactly one weight function.
    # We can check this from fws_list_reactive()
    p_value_boundary_shown <- FALSE
    if (K_analyses > 0) {
      all_single_weights <- all(sapply(fws_list_reactive(), length) == 1)
      if (all_single_weights) {
        p_value_boundary_shown <- TRUE
        num_plots_expected <- num_plots_expected + 1
      }
    }

    # Determine grid layout
    if (num_plots_expected <= 9) {
      rows <- 3
      cols <- 3
    } else if (num_plots_expected <= 12) {
      rows <- 4
      cols <- 3
    } else {
      rows <- ceiling(sqrt(num_plots_expected))
      cols <- ceiling(num_plots_expected / rows)
      showNotification(
        paste("Many plots generated (", num_plots_expected, "). Layout may be crowded."),
        type = "warning", duration = 5
      )
    }

    op <- par(mfrow = c(rows, cols), oma = c(0, 0, 3, 0), mar = c(4, 4, 2, 1) + 0.1)
    on.exit(par(op)) # Restore graphical parameters on exit

    # CRITICAL RULE: BOUNDARY CORRECTNESS (KB 14.9.A)
    # display.nphDesign calls f.extract() internally; the object from
    # finalize.nphDesign is assumed compatible. Verify with str() on the
    # constructor output if this errors.
    nphDesign::display.nphDesign(nphDesign = nphDesign_object(), maxT = input$maxT_display)

    mtext("NPH Study Design Overview", side = 3, line = 1, outer = TRUE, cex = 1.5, font = 2)
  }, res = 96) # res controls DPI for better plot quality
}

shinyApp(ui, server)
